target: CAT-005 Recommended Proposal Package — V51.1 Refraction Cascade
topic: Proposal-package review: new Section 1.7, cascade_dependent tag, twelve-WIN verdict flip table
gate_decision: Analyst may NOT proceed to Section 1.7 prose until the blockers below are cleared. Structural concept is approved; verdict-flip table and schema change are NOT yet approved.
what_is_approved: The core analytical claim — that V51.1's physical/apparent sun altitude split is load-bearing and cascades into multiple WIN-level self-contradictions — is sound and well-argued. The kernel of truth narrative (genuine engineering attempt, not a lazy fudge) hits the right tone for our review.

Structural placement recommendation (Option a): new subsection under Part 1, after 1.5 Aetheric Refraction and 1.6 Dielectric Foundation. This is the correct home for a cross-cutting geometric argument.

The seven-point section outline is well-sequenced: fix → required angle → n(r) inconsistency → universal displacement → seasonal modulation → eclipse geometry → self-defeating fix. Points 2, 3, and 7 are the strongest.
The decision to keep all existing data rebuttals as secondary evidence (retention_policy) is correct and preserves the review's defense-in-depth.
Defense 3 (moving target critique) is correctly identified as the strongest dome rebuttal and the analyst's response — make the argument structural rather than version-specific — is right.
Deferring the numerics reproduction to a separate EXP-050-numerics.json companion and requiring curmudgeon line-by-line validation is the right process.

blockers_before_prose.description: The verdict flip table is unreliable because the analyst explicitly did not re-read each affected WIN's detail_evidence before proposing flips (see risk #1 in section 6). Spot check: WIN-010 is described in wins.json as 'BOU 2017 eclipse -10.9 nT' — a geomagnetic measurement during an eclipse, NOT a solar analemma or daylight-hours prediction as the analyst guessed ('If WIN-010 is the solar analemma or daylight-hours prediction (needs verification)'). The cascade's Point 2 (required refraction angle) and Point 5 (seasonal modulation) do not cleanly reach WIN-010's claim about BOU magnetometer readings. Similarly, WIN-017 is 'Parallax = firmament wobble' — stellar parallax refuted by Gaia microarcsecond precision — not 'triangulation-based position claims' as the analyst guessed. The cascade touches stellar positions via Point 3 (n(r) inconsistency) and Point 4 (universal displacement), not via triangulation baselines.
blockers_before_prose.required_action: Analyst MUST perform the pre-commit audit listed in risk #1 BEFORE any Section 1.7 prose is written. Re-read each of the twelve WINs' current detail_claim, detail_evidence, and detail_verdict_text. For each, classify the cascade's contact surface as (a) primary self-contradiction, (b) secondary cross-reference only, or (c) no contact — flip withdrawn. Produce a revised flip table and resubmit for curmudgeon sign-off before section prose.

blockers_before_prose.description: Two proposed flips would downgrade strong 'Refuted by Data' verdicts to 'Self-Contradicted'. WIN-017 (parallax, refuted by Gaia DR3 1.8B stars at microarcsecond precision) and WIN-026 (crepuscular ray divergence, refuted by direct observation geometry) are among our cleanest data refutations. Flipping them to Self-Contradicted trades an airtight data kill for a geometric argument that is less directly observable and still depends on the cascade's own numerics holding. A hostile reader will interpret the downgrade as 'the reviewer no longer has data on their side.' This is a rhetorical loss, not a gain.
blockers_before_prose.required_action: For WIN-017 and WIN-026, default to 'stay put, cite cascade as supplementary' unless the analyst can show that the cascade argument is strictly stronger than the existing data refutation on its own terms. The burden of proof is on the flip.

blockers_before_prose.description: The proposed dual-label verdict 'Refuted by Data + Self-Contradicted (dual)' for WIN-016 is not supported by the review's verdict taxonomy. Every WIN carries exactly one verdict. The analyst knows this ('If dual labels are not supported, stay with Refuted by Data'). Proposing a schema-breaking verdict as the primary recommendation, even with a fallback, muddies the decision and will force the decider into a messy patch conversation. Withdraw the dual label entirely.
blockers_before_prose.required_action: Replace the WIN-016 row with 'stay put — Refuted by Data, cite cascade as supplementary in detail_evidence.' Do not touch the verdict field.

blockers_before_prose.description: The numerics — 1,975–3,618 km physical altitude range, 13–27° refraction angle, 400–675× n(r) ratio, 83% angular-diameter modulation, 3–8× tidal modulation — are load-bearing for the entire Section 1.7 argument but have not been reproduced from current raw-text/ files. If any of these numbers is wrong, the cascade collapses. The analyst's own risk #4 and risk #8 acknowledge this. Curmudgeon sanity check: for physical h=1975 km and observer horizontal distance 5000 km, apparent (5733 km) bending is ~27°; for h=3618 km and d=5000 km, bending is ~13°. The 13–27° range is plausible in rough geometry, but the analyst has not shown the sub-solar-point / latitude geometry that produces the claimed range across real observer locations, and the lower bound may actually be closer to 9° for far-from-sub-solar observers.
blockers_before_prose.required_action: EXP-050-numerics.json must be produced and validated BEFORE Section 1.7 prose is written. It must contain: (a) the exact V51.1 physical altitude formula copied from raw-text/ with file and line reference, (b) the n(r) formula with file reference, (c) a worked computation showing sunlight bending at three representative observer geometries (sub-solar, mid-latitude, high-latitude), (d) a worked computation showing starlight bending using the same n(r) at the same radii, (e) a worked seasonal angular-diameter computation with the globe baseline for comparison, (f) a worked tidal-force-ratio computation. Each number that will appear in the section prose must trace to a line in this companion file.

blockers_before_prose.description: The proposed new code_analysis tag 'cascade_dependent' is a category error. The code_analysis field describes structural facts about the DOME model (monitoring methodology, relabeling, post-hoc adoption, geometric derivation). Whether a WIN's detail_evidence cross-references Section 1.7 is a fact about OUR review's editorial structure, not about the dome. Mixing editorial metadata into code_analysis muddies the schema and sets a precedent future reviewers will extend badly. It also triggers unnecessary schema-change blast radius (test.js assertions, generate-html.js placeholders, backfill pass) for a tag that doesn't carry dome-structural information.
blockers_before_prose.required_action: Do NOT add cascade_dependent to code_analysis. Instead, use one of these alternatives: (a) add a free-form 'cross_references' array at the WIN top level listing section IDs the WIN cites, (b) enumerate cascade-dependent WINs in Section 1.7 itself via a {{CASCADE_WIN_LIST}} placeholder computed at build time from detail_evidence substring match, or (c) simply hard-list the twelve WINs in the Section 1.7 prose and skip the schema entirely. Option (c) is the lowest-blast-radius path for a first implementation.

blockers_before_prose.description: Proposed section title — '1.7 The V51.1 Refraction Cascade: How One Parameter Fix Breaks Everything' — is version-coupled. The analyst's own response to Defense 3 correctly argues the section should be written structurally so it survives V52. A version-stamped title undermines that framing. The same objection applies, though less strongly, to the 'v51_cascade' tag name option that was already rejected — but the section title was not subjected to the same rigor.
blockers_before_prose.required_action: Rename to a structural title, e.g. 'The Refraction Cascade: Why the Sun/Firmament Fix Creates More Contradictions Than It Resolves' or 'The Sun-Above-Ceiling Cascade.' Mention V51.1 as the specific instance in the first paragraph, not in the section heading.

blockers_before_prose.description: The proposal names the placement as 'Section 1.7' but does not verify the current numbering in data/sections.json. Part 1 currently has keys part1 and part1b (post-V6 restructure), and the actual subsection numbering inside each part is determined by the prose, not a dedicated key. A hard-coded '1.7' label risks collision or renumbering churn if Part 1 subsections are reordered. The restructure map shows V6 already renumbered everything once; we should not bake in a new number before confirming it fits.
blockers_before_prose.required_action: Before writing prose, open data/sections.json and confirm the current highest subsection number in part1 or part1b. Propose the new section as a labelled subsection inside the appropriate existing key rather than as a new top-level key, unless the section is long enough (>800 words) to justify its own key.

holes_found.description: Point 6 (Eclipse Geometry Paradox) claims 'the cascade leaves the physical altitude underdetermined (only a seasonal range, not a single value per eclipse)'. This is asserted, not demonstrated. The dome's V51.1 patch may in fact assign a specific physical altitude per date via a seasonal formula — in which case the 'underdetermined' framing is wrong and the argument becomes weaker. The analyst has not cited a dome source that shows the altitude is genuinely left open per eclipse.
holes_found.recommendation: Either find the dome formula/table that assigns physical altitude by date (and then argue the per-date values contradict NASA eclipse paths), or drop Point 6 and reduce the outline to six points. Do not leave an unsupported 'underdetermined' claim in the section.

holes_found.description: Point 4 (Universal Displacement) argues the moon would need an even larger bending because it's at lower apparent altitude (2,534 km) than the sun (5,733 km). But if the dome's V51.1 patch applies its aetheric refraction uniformly, the moon's physical altitude should also be split, and the cascade argument needs to spell out what the moon's physical altitude is in the patched model. Silently conflating 'apparent altitude' with 'physical altitude' for the moon while explicitly separating them for the sun is exactly the rhetorical move we accuse the dome of making.
holes_found.recommendation: In Point 4, explicitly state whether V51.1 applies the physical/apparent split to the moon. If yes, compute the moon's required refraction angle using the same method as the sun. If no, argue that the selective application of the split is itself a dome self-inconsistency.

holes_found.description: Defense 2 response is not airtight. The analyst says observables 'that depend on the physical altitude' include tidal force, angular diameter, and eclipse shadow geometry. But the dome defender can rebut: 'Aetheric refraction bends all electromagnetic signals including the gravitational carrier; the physical position is unobservable in principle; only the apparent position matters because all physics is mediated through the aether.' This is a Berkeleyan move — unfalsifiable by construction — and our response has to invoke Section 1.5's unfalsifiability framing explicitly, not just assert 'observed tidal amplitudes match globe predictions.'
holes_found.recommendation: Strengthen the Defense 2 response to explicitly tie back to Section 1.5 unfalsifiability and note that once the dome admits aetheric refraction mediates gravity as well as light, it has abandoned any claim to make discriminating predictions.

holes_found.description: The executive summary says the cascade ties 'twelve WIN-level rebuttals into a single structural self-contradiction.' After blockers B1–B3 are cleared, the number of cleanly-flipped WINs will likely be smaller than twelve. The summary number will be wrong and should not be locked in until the revised flip table exists.
holes_found.recommendation: Leave the count as a {{CASCADE_WIN_COUNT}} placeholder or rewrite the summary after the revised flip table is approved.

holes_found.description: '400-675x n(r) ratio' and '83% angular modulation' are presented as ranges without uncertainty quantification. Readers trained in physics will want error bars or at least an explicit note that these are order-of-magnitude characterizations.
holes_found.recommendation: In the numerics file (B4), show the arithmetic and label values as 'order of magnitude' or 'approximate' where appropriate.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The V51.1 refraction cascade is a critique of a work in progress. The dome model author is publicly iterating, acknowledging the sun/firmament collision, and attempting a principled fix. By building an entire section around the specific numerics of a V51.1 patch, the review chains itself to a moving target and will have to rewrite Section 1.7 whenever the author releases V52. Worse, the review's length grows faster than the dome's — every new dome patch triggers a new cascade section — which eventually makes the review unmaintainable. The mature move is to point at the general impossibility (no flat-disc geometry with the dome's other constraints can fit the sun below the ceiling) and decline to engage with the specific V51.1 arithmetic, which is transient by design.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Section 1.7 must open with the structural statement and treat V51.1 as an illustrative instance. The section's first paragraph should explicitly say: 'The specific numerics in this section are drawn from V51.1 as of [date]. The argument does not depend on V51.1 specifically — any flat-disc cosmology with the dome's firmament ceiling function must produce a cascade of the same structural shape. If a future version revises the sun altitude or firmament function, the cascade re-instantiates with new numbers and the same conclusion.' The section title, tag name, and placeholder machinery should all be version-agnostic (this is Blocker B6). The EXP-050-numerics.json file should be structured so that a future curmudgeon can re-run the arithmetic against new raw-text/ data without touching the prose.

cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-044 (Firmament scaling) and WIN-056 (Solar elevation from H(r)) are the cascade's natural anchors — their existing arguments already lean on the same geometry. WIN-046 (Tidal S2) and WIN-067 (Antarctic gravity hole) are Self-Contradicted and the cascade adds a coherent cross-reference. No internal contradictions between the cascade argument and these existing rebuttals. The risks are all in the moderate-confidence flips (010, 016, 017, 018, 019, 026, 068) — flagged in Blockers B1–B3.

claims_checked: 13-27° refraction angle range for physical altitude 1975-3618 km at observer distance 5000 km
Refraction range sensitivity to observer distance
quantitative_verification.computations: Spot check using simple flat-disc geometry: arctan(h_apparent/d) − arctan(h_physical/d). For h_phys=1975, d=5000: delta=27.4°. For h_phys=3618, d=5000: delta=13.0°. At larger observer distances (d=12000), delta drops to 16.2° and 8.8° respectively. The analyst's 13-27° characterization is plausible at mid-range observer distances but the lower bound may extend below 13° for far-from-sub-solar observers. This is a ballpark check only — the full computation with spherical or latitude-sensitive geometry is deferred to EXP-050-numerics.json.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The 13-27° range is defensible as an 'order of magnitude' characterization but not as a hard bound. The lower bound in particular may be too high by a factor of ~1.5 at high-latitude observer positions. This does not refute the cascade — even 9° bending is three orders of magnitude beyond real atmospheric refraction — but the numbers in the section prose should match the companion-file arithmetic exactly or the hostile reader will pounce.
quantitative_verification.n_r_starlight_ratio_unchecked: The 400-675x n(r) ratio for starlight vs sunlight bending was NOT verified in this review — curmudgeon did not locate the n(r) formula in the current raw-text/ clone during this run. This is in scope for EXP-050-numerics.json.

kernel_of_truth.description: The analyst has identified something structurally important. The V51.1 split IS the dome model's most telling move: it is an honest attempt to patch a real contradiction, and the attempt itself creates the cascade. That framing — 'the patch is a serious engineering attempt that fails for structural reasons' — is the right tone and is the correct rhetorical position for our review. This is not a cheap takedown; it credits the author for trying and explains why the try cannot succeed.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Recognizing the kernel does not mean approving the proposal as written. The concept is strong; the execution has verification gaps, schema mistakes, and rhetorical overreach in the verdict flip table. Fix those and the section becomes the review's centerpiece. Ship them as-is and Section 1.7 becomes a liability that hostile readers will use to discredit the rest of the review.

citations_unchecked: Meeus Astronomical Algorithms ch. 16 (0.5° max atmospheric refraction)
Gaia DR3 1.8B stars microarcsecond precision (consistent with WIN-016 citation)
update_optical_caveats.py — dome repo, referenced but not quoted
citation_check.note: Citation verification is not required at the proposal-package stage, but all of the above must be verified when the Section 1.7 prose is drafted and when EXP-050-numerics.json is produced.

accept: Placement in Part 1 after 1.5/1.6 (subject to Blocker B7 subsection-numbering check)
Retention policy for existing data rebuttals as secondary evidence
modify: Point 4 (Universal Displacement) → make moon treatment explicit
Point 6 (Eclipse Geometry Paradox) → find the dome's per-date altitude assignment or drop the point
Advocate Defense 2 response → tie back to Section 1.5 unfalsifiability framing
reject_or_defer: Verdict flip table as written — blocked on B1 until per-WIN audit is done
WIN-017 and WIN-026 flips from Refuted by Data to Self-Contradicted (B2)
Writing Section 1.7 prose before EXP-050-numerics.json exists and is validated (B4)

other_side_counter.note: Per the standing 'think about the other side' instruction, here is the strongest case AGAINST my own recommendation to modify rather than accept.
other_side_counter.case: The blockers listed could be read as risk-aversion dressed up as rigor. The analyst's proposal is substantively correct: the cascade is real, the numerics are defensible, the flips are mostly right. By demanding a pre-commit audit, a numerics companion file, a schema rewrite, and a title change, the curmudgeon is adding four gates to a proposal that the review needs urgently — the dome's V51.1 release is recent and the cascade argument is time-sensitive in terms of public framing. Over-gating delays a major improvement and risks the decider losing momentum.
other_side_counter.response: The time-sensitivity argument has some force but does not override verification. WIN-010 is genuinely misidentified in the flip table — the analyst guessed 'analemma or daylight hours' and it's actually 'BOU 2017 eclipse -10.9 nT.' That is a factual error the analyst caught itself (risk #1) and flagged as pre-commit blocker material. Shipping Section 1.7 with that error would be exactly the kind of 'the critics didn't do their homework' gift to the dome defender that the curmudgeon exists to prevent. The schema rejection (B5) is the one blocker that might be pure rigor — the code_analysis tag is low-cost and the section would work with it — but the category-error argument still holds and the proposed alternatives cost nothing. Net assessment: the gates are load-bearing, not decorative.

next_steps_for_analyst: Do not write Section 1.7 prose yet.
Produce EXP-050-numerics.json with the worked arithmetic described in Blocker B4.

Perform the per-WIN audit described in Blocker B1 for the seven moderate-confidence flips (010, 016, 017, 018, 019, 026, 068). Re-read each WIN's current detail_evidence and classify cascade contact surface as primary / secondary / none.
Revise the flip table per B2 (WIN-017, WIN-026 default stay-put) and B3 (WIN-016 stay-put, no dual label).
Drop the cascade_dependent code_analysis tag (B5). Plan to hard-list cascade-dependent WINs in Section 1.7 prose itself or use a top-level cross_references array.
Rename section to a version-agnostic title (B6).
Confirm Part 1 subsection numbering against data/sections.json before proposing a specific section number (B7).
Resubmit as EXP-050 revision 2 for a second curmudgeon gate pass.
